efficiency_issues.location: marketing-instagram-squad: All agents load 9 marketing frameworks
efficiency_issues.description: Each of 5 agents in marketing-instagram-squad duplicates framework references in squad.yaml (StoryBrand, Value Equation, Hook-Story-Offer, AIDA, STEPPS, AARRR, etc.). These frameworks appear across agent definitions but are never referenced in actual task implementations.
efficiency_issues.impact: Context bloat: ~5K tokens per squad load for frameworks never used. Knowledge duplication makes framework updates require changes in 5 places instead of 1.
efficiency_issues.example: squad.yaml lines 29-50 list frameworks in agent definitions, but agents/profile-strategist.md never references them by name
efficiency_issues.fix: Create single frameworks-registry.md in squad root. Reference by ID in agents. Load framework details on-demand, not at squad initialization.

efficiency_issues.description: Strategy document is created in launch-new-profile (Day 1) and updated in optimize-existing-profile (Week 2). However, neither workflow validates whether the update is actually backwards-compatible or tracks what changed. New tests in suggest-adjustments depend on strategy changes, but there's no explicit handoff documenting what changed.
efficiency_issues.impact: Tests may be misaligned with updated strategy. Requires re-reading entire strategy doc to understand deltas. If strategy-document gets large (10KB+), context window gets expensive.
efficiency_issues.example: optimize-existing-profile.md Week 2 says 'Validar: core messages ainda ressoam?' but doesn't create a changelog. suggest-adjustments.md (Week 1 output) must re-read entire strategy to find changes.
efficiency_issues.fix: Add optional output in update-strategy task: strategy-changelog.md showing [REMOVED], [UPDATED], [ADDED] for core-messages, pillars, hooks. Reference changelog in suggest-adjustments inputs.

efficiency_issues.description: Days 2, 3, 4 are strictly sequential (copywriter → planner → researcher), but many tasks have no true dependency. Copywriter writes 5 captions in Day 2 (3 hrs). Content planner creates calendar in Day 3 (2 hrs morning). But planner could start calendar in parallel with copywriter Day 2 afternoon, since both need strategy doc (available since Day 1).
efficiency_issues.impact: 5-day workflow could compress to 4 days. ~20% longer than necessary. If scaling to 10 profiles, cumulative overhead is 2+ days.
efficiency_issues.example: Day 2: @copywriter write-caption (3hrs) vs Day 3: @content-planner create-calendar (2hrs). Planner's input is strategy-doc (from Day 1) + pilar %. Planner doesn't need 5 captions to exist before starting skeleton calendar.
efficiency_issues.fix: Refactor as: Day 2: copywriter (3hrs) + planner start calendar structure (1hr parallel). Day 3: planner finish calendar (1hr). Overlaps 1hr, saves 1 full day.

efficiency_issues.description: Both workflows have Rio (responsive specialist) audit at the end, and both have Nova run a11y + performance audits independently. However, responsive-audit (Rio) and a11y-audit (Nova) both check focus rings, color contrast, and mobile layouts. 20-30% of checks overlap.
efficiency_issues.impact: Redundant testing. If a focus ring issue is found by Rio, Nova will find it again. Time waste: ~2-3 hours per component.
efficiency_issues.example: component-creation.md Day 3: Rio tests 'All interactive elements accessible' + Nova tests 'Screen reader + keyboard nav'. Both test keyboard navigation independently.
efficiency_issues.fix: Create unified responsive-a11y-audit.md combining both into single pass. Rio owns responsive at all breakpoints, Nova owns a11y compliance. Single document, coordinated checklist.

efficiency_issues.description: squad.yaml (line 136) lists dependencies.frameworks-extraction and dependencies.persona-instructions, but tasks never reference these files. profile-context.yaml exists but is mentioned only in define-strategy.md inputs as 'partially predefilled' with no guidance on what fields.
efficiency_issues.impact: Configuration files loaded at initialization but never accessed. Wasted I/O and memory. Maintenance risk: if framework paths change, squad still tries to load old paths.
efficiency_issues.example: squad.yaml line 136 'frameworks-extraction: ../.aiox/frameworks-extraction.md' but no agent task actually imports or reads this file.
efficiency_issues.fix: Remove unused dependencies from squad.yaml. If framework extraction is needed, add explicit task input. Clean up profile-context.yaml or document required fields with schema.

efficiency_issues.location: marketing-instagram-squad: All workflows have handoff sections
efficiency_issues.description: Each workflow handoff block (launch-new-profile Days 1-5, optimize-existing-profile Weeks 1-4) includes 'Pass to:' sections with full context repeated. Example: Day 1 handoff lists all 5 core messages, all 10 hooks, etc. Day 2 could infer these from strategy-doc link.
efficiency_issues.impact: Handoff sections add 500+ words per workflow. Not critical, but reduces scannability. Reduces clarity (readers must match handoff to actual document).
efficiency_issues.example: launch-new-profile.md line 29-37: handoff repeats '5 core messages: [lista]' when reader can just look at {handle}-strategy-document.md.
efficiency_issues.fix: Simplify handoff format to: 'Pass to: @copywriter. Previous output: {handle}-strategy-document.md. Next task: write-caption.' Remove context summaries; link to source of truth.

efficiency_issues.location: visual-design-squad: responsive-audit.md and a11y-audit.md missing explicit blockers
efficiency_issues.description: responsive-audit.md and a11y-audit.md both list 'requires: frontend-implement-page' in dependencies, but the task YAML doesn't declare 'blocks' relationships. This means if frontend-implement-page has a bug, Rio and Nova start auditing incomplete code, waste hours, then must re-audit.
efficiency_issues.impact: No task orchestration. Agents don't know which task waits for them. Manual coordination required.
efficiency_issues.example: responsive-audit.md (lines 42-44) says 'requires: frontend-implement-page' but doesn't declare 'blocks: [performance-audit, quality-gate-visual]'. This inverse relationship must be inferred.
efficiency_issues.fix: Add 'blocks' field in responsive-audit and a11y-audit task YAML. Declare: 'responsive-audit blocks: quality-gate-visual' so runner knows order.

efficiency_issues.location: marketing-instagram-squad and visual-design-squad both define personas using external frameworks
efficiency_issues.description: marketing-instagram-squad agents reference frameworks like 'StoryBrand 7-Point (Donald Miller)'. visual-design-squad agents reference 'Atomic Design (Brad Frost)', 'Design of Everyday Things (Norman)'. Both load from same persona integration points ('../.aiox/personas/') but define frameworks separately in squad.yaml.
efficiency_issues.impact: If framework list changes, both squads must be updated. No single source of truth. If adding a new framework, unclear which squad 'owns' it.
efficiency_issues.example: marketing-instagram-squad/squad.yaml defines frameworks in agent blocks. visual-design-squad/squad.yaml does the same. No deduplicated registry.
efficiency_issues.fix: Create central .aiox/frameworks-registry.yaml (or extend existing one) with all frameworks. Both squads reference by ID: 'frameworks: [storybrand-7pt, value-equation-hormozi]' instead of full definitions.

efficiency_issues.type: Template Output Formats Not Linked to Tasks
efficiency_issues.location: marketing-instagram-squad: templates/ folder has markdown templates, but tasks don't explicitly reference them
efficiency_issues.description: marketing-instagram-squad/templates/ has caption-output.md, content-calendar.md, strategy-document.md, etc. But tasks (write-caption.md, create-calendar.md) embed their own format examples instead of linking to template files. This creates two sources of truth.
efficiency_issues.impact: If template format changes, task example must be manually updated. Risk of divergence.
efficiency_issues.example: write-caption.md (lines 48-96) embeds full caption-*.md structure. But templates/caption-output.md likely has the same format. Two places to maintain.
efficiency_issues.fix: Tasks should say 'Output: Use template {squad}/templates/caption-output.md and substitute [variables]'. Maintain one source (template file), tasks reference it.

efficiency_issues.description: @analytics-agent is the only agent who can run analyze-performance and suggest-adjustments. In optimize-existing-profile, Week 1 (5 hours) + Week 4 (2 hours). If two profiles need optimization in same month, @analytics-agent is blocked for 7+ hours before @profile-strategist can even start Week 2.
efficiency_issues.impact: Cannot scale to >2-3 simultaneous profiles without bottleneck. Week 2+ agents (strategist, copywriter, planner) idle waiting for analytics.
efficiency_issues.example: optimize-existing-profile.md Week 1: 'Monday-Friday (5 horas)' — @analytics-agent alone. Weeks 2-4 agents idle until done.
efficiency_issues.fix: Consider task splitting: analyze-performance (data collection) vs suggest-adjustments (strategy). Allow parallel work on data analysis and strategy review. Or document acceptable load: max 2 concurrent profiles per month.

efficiency_issues.type: Large Output Documents Passed Between Tasks
efficiency_issues.description: strategy-document.md includes StoryBrand 7-point (full section), Value Equation (full section), 10 Hook Frameworks (detailed examples), Content Pillars (descriptions), 5 Core Messages, and Operational Recommendations. Estimated 2-3KB per profile. Every downstream task (write-caption, create-calendar, suggest-adjustments) loads this entire document, even if using only 1 section (e.g., copywriter only needs hook frameworks + core messages).
efficiency_issues.impact: Context window inflation. If strategy grows to 5KB, and 8 tasks reference it, cumulative load is 40KB. For 10 profiles, 400KB+ of redundant context.
efficiency_issues.example: write-caption.md inputs say 'Precisa de: 1. {handle}-strategy-document.md' — full doc. But task only uses core messages + hooks (maybe 200 lines of 800).
efficiency_issues.fix: Create modular strategy artifacts: core-messages.md (50 lines), hooks-framework.md (300 lines), content-pillars.md (100 lines). Tasks load only needed modules.

unused_components.description: Referenced in squad.yaml but never imported or documented. No task reads it. Unclear purpose.
unused_components.recommendation: Document schema with required fields, or remove if unused.

unused_components.description: Both squads reference frameworks-extraction.md in dependencies, but file doesn't exist in repo (presumed missing or in parent .aiox dir). Creates broken reference.
unused_components.recommendation: Either create frameworks-extraction.md or remove from dependencies.

unused_components.description: Templates exist but tasks embed duplicate examples instead of linking to templates. Templates become stale.
unused_components.recommendation: Update tasks to reference templates by name. Remove embedded examples from task descriptions.

unused_components.description: In component-creation.md workflow, Iris is listed as participant but has no component-specific tasks. Iris only appears in page-to-production.md (Day 3: Interaction Phase). In component-creation, interactions are left to Nova's quality gate.
unused_components.recommendation: Either remove Iris from component-creation or add interaction-design task for components (currently skipped).

optimization_opportunities.title: Parallelize Marketing Days 2-4
optimization_opportunities.description: Copywriter (Day 2) and Content Planner (Day 3) have no hard dependency on each other. Both need strategy-doc (ready Day 1). Planner can start calendar skeleton while copywriter finishes captions.
optimization_opportunities.implementation: Refactor launch-new-profile.md: Add 'Day 2.5' section showing copywriter finishing captions + planner starting calendar in parallel. Adjust handoff timing.

optimization_opportunities.title: Consolidate Responsive + A11y Audits
optimization_opportunities.current_timeline: Rio audits, then Nova audits (sequential or partial overlap)
optimization_opportunities.proposed_timeline: Single unified audit, Rio + Nova coordinate once
optimization_opportunities.description: Rio (responsive) and Nova (a11y/performance) both test keyboard nav, color contrast, touch targets. Combine into single pass with coordinated checklist.
optimization_opportunities.implementation: Create unified responsive-a11y-audit.md task that both agents run together. Divide responsibilities: Rio owns layout/images, Nova owns focus/contrast/keyboard. Both sign off on single report.

optimization_opportunities.title: Modularize Strategy Document
optimization_opportunities.area: marketing-instagram-squad: All tasks depend on strategy-document
optimization_opportunities.proposed_timeline: Tasks load 200-300 byte module specific to their needs
optimization_opportunities.description: Split strategy-document.md into: core-messages.md, hooks-framework.md, content-pillars.md, operational-rec.md. Tasks reference only needed modules.
optimization_opportunities.implementation: Refactor define-strategy output to create 4 modular files instead of 1 big doc. Update all task inputs to reference specific modules.

optimization_opportunities.title: Create Framework Registry
optimization_opportunities.description: Instead of listing frameworks in squad.yaml agent blocks, create central registry. Squads reference by ID. Single source of truth.
optimization_opportunities.effort_savings: Maintenance time: 50% reduction per framework update
optimization_opportunities.implementation: Create .aiox/frameworks-registry.yaml with entries like 'storybrand-7pt: {source, description, application}'. Agents reference by ID only.

optimization_opportunities.title: Simplify Workflow Handoff Sections
optimization_opportunities.current_timeline: Handoff blocks include context summary (5-10 lines each)
optimization_opportunities.proposed_timeline: Handoff blocks reference previous outputs (1-2 lines each)
optimization_opportunities.description: Instead of repeating '5 core messages: [list]' in handoff, say 'Previous output: {handle}-strategy-document.md (see core messages section)'. Readers know where truth is.
optimization_opportunities.effort_savings: Reduce workflow doc size 15%, improve scannability
optimization_opportunities.implementation: Update all 'Handoff to' sections: replace context summaries with 'Previous output: [filename]' links.

optimization_opportunities.title: Add Task Blockers in YAML
optimization_opportunities.description: Add inverse dependency: responsive-audit.md should declare 'blocks: [quality-gate-visual]'. Allows task orchestration engine to know order.
optimization_opportunities.effort_savings: Enable parallel execution planning, reduce manual coordination
optimization_opportunities.implementation: Add 'blocks: [task-ids]' field to responsive-audit.md, a11y-audit.md, performance-audit.md YAML headers.

optimization_opportunities.title: Document Load Profile by Squad Size
optimization_opportunities.current_timeline: No documented scaling limits or bottlenecks
optimization_opportunities.proposed_timeline: Document max concurrent profiles based on agent availability
optimization_opportunities.description: Add section to optimize-existing-profile.md: 'Scaling: @analytics-agent is bottleneck. Recommend max 2 concurrent profiles per month. For 3+, hire second analyst.'
optimization_opportunities.implementation: Add 'Scaling & Capacity' section to both workflow docs listing agent bottlenecks and max concurrent workload.

optimization_opportunities.title: Create Delta Tracking for Strategy Updates
optimization_opportunities.current_timeline: Update-strategy task doesn't output what changed
optimization_opportunities.description: When @profile-strategist updates strategy (Week 2), output a changelog: [REMOVED] core messages, [UPDATED] pillars, [ADDED] hooks. Downstream tasks (suggest-adjustments) reference changelog instead of re-reading entire doc.
optimization_opportunities.effort_savings: Reduce context load for downstream tasks, faster decision-making
optimization_opportunities.implementation: Add optional output to update-strategy task: 'strategy-delta-{mes-ano}.md' with [REMOVED], [UPDATED], [ADDED] sections.

optimization_opportunities.title: Link Templates to Tasks Explicitly
optimization_opportunities.current_timeline: Tasks embed format examples; templates folder unused
optimization_opportunities.proposed_timeline: Tasks reference template files by path
optimization_opportunities.description: Tasks (write-caption.md, create-calendar.md) should say 'Output: Use templates/caption-output.md as format' instead of embedding example structure.
optimization_opportunities.effort_savings: Reduce task doc size 20%, single source of truth
optimization_opportunities.implementation: Update task outputs section: 'Entregar arquivo {nome} using template squads/marketing-instagram-squad/templates/{template-name}.md'.

optimization_opportunities.title: Add Interaction Design to Component Workflow
optimization_opportunities.proposed_timeline: Component workflow: Design (Stella) → Interaction (Iris) → Implement (Claude) → Test (Rio/Nova)
optimization_opportunities.description: page-to-production.md includes Iris (Day 3) for interaction specs. component-creation.md should also include Iris between design and implementation. Currently skipped, causing incomplete state specs.
optimization_opportunities.effort_savings: Ensures components have complete state definitions, reduces QA rework
optimization_opportunities.implementation: Add Day 1.5 to component-creation.md: Iris creates interaction-spec.md for component states/animations before Claude implements.

summary.optimization_potential: 20-30% efficiency gain with implementation of top 5 optimizations
key_findings: Framework definitions are duplicated across agents and squads, creating maintenance burden
5-day marketing workflow could compress to 4 days via parallelization
Context bloat from passing large documents (strategy-doc) could be reduced 70% with modularization
Visual design squad has redundant a11y/responsive audits (20-30% overlap)
Analytics agent is bottleneck for optimize-existing-profile; scales to max 2-3 concurrent profiles
Task dependencies are undeclared; no explicit 'blocks' relationships for orchestration
Workflow handoff sections are verbose; could be simplified by linking to documents
Templates exist but are shadowed by embedded task examples; no single source of truth
